verses.2: “Oh if only my grief were actually weighed And laid in the balances together with my disaster!
verses.3: “For then it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; For that reason my words have been rash.
verses.4: “For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, My spirit drinks their poison; The terrors of God line up against me.
verses.5: “Does the wild donkey bray over his grass, Or does the ox low over his feed?
verses.6: “Can something tasteless be eaten without salt, Or is there any taste in the juice of an alkanet plant?
verses.7: “My soul refuses to touch them; They are like loathsome food to me.
verses.8: “Oh, that my request might come to pass, And that God would grant my hope!
verses.9: “ Oh, that God would decide to crush me, That He would let loose His hand and cut me off!
verses.10: “But it is still my comfort, And I rejoice in unsparing pain, That I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
verses.11: “What is my strength, that I should wait? And what is my end, that I should endure?
verses.12: “Is my strength the strength of stones, Or is my flesh bronze?
verses.13: “Is it that my help is not within me, And that a good outcome is driven away from me?
verses.14: “For the despairing man there should be kindness from his friend; So that he does not abandon the fear of the Almighty.
verses.15: “My brothers have acted deceitfully like a wadi, Like the torrents of wadis which drain away,
verses.16: Which are darkened because of ice, And into which the snow melts.
verses.17: “When they dry up, they vanish; When it is hot, they disappear from their place.
verses.18: “The paths of their course wind along, They go up into wasteland and perish.
verses.19: “The caravans of Tema looked, The travelers of Sheba hoped for them.
verses.20: “They were put to shame, for they had trusted, They came there and were humiliated.
verses.21: “Indeed, you have now become such, You see terrors and are afraid.
verses.22: “Have I said, ‘Give me something, ’ Or, ‘Offer a bribe for me from your wealth,’
verses.23: Or, ‘Save me from the hand of the enemy,’ Or, ‘Redeem me from the hand of the tyrants’?
verses.24: “Teach me, and I will be silent; And show me how I have done wrong.
verses.25: “How painful are honest words! But what does your argument prove?
verses.26: “Do you intend to rebuke my words, When the words of one in despair belong to the wind?
verses.27: “You would even cast lots for the orphans, And barter over your friend.
verses.28: “Now please look at me, And see if I am lying to your face.
verses.29: “Please turn away, let there be no injustice; Turn away, my righteousness is still in it.
verses.30: “Is there injustice on my tongue? Does my palate not discern disasters?
